question.text: Under the PFUTP Regulations, SEBI can impound and retain proceeds or securities in respect of a transaction which is:

answer.text: In violation or prima facie in violation of the regulations
Only when the amount exceeds Rs. 1 crore
Only in confirmed violation of the regulations
Only after the final order has been passed

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI can impound and retain the proceeds or securities in respect of any transaction which is in violation OR PRIMA FACIE in violation of the regulations. **The Trap:** The 'prima facie' threshold is lower than confirmed violation - SEBI can act even based on preliminary evidence. **Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI's interim powers are tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Prima facie evidence is sufficient for interim orders - this prevents fraudsters from dissipating ill-gotten gains during lengthy investigations.
